description: Named MCP exposure profiles control which tools and resources the AINL MCP server registers at startup. They scope what MCP hosts can discover and call. They do NOT replace security profiles (runtime adapter restrictions) or capability grants (execution authorization). The optional a2a (Agent-to-Agent) network adapter is not enabled by exposure profile alone: turn it on under server/MCP config adapters (adapters.enable + adapters.a2a); see docs/integrations/A2A_ADAPTER.md.

validate_only.description: Read-only validation and compilation; no execution, security-report, or resources. Recommended for ultra-narrow validation-only usage (e.g. desktop-bound or Cowork/Dispatch-style agents).

inspect_only.description: Read-only inspection: validation, compilation, capabilities, and security reports; no execution. Recommended default for desktop-bound, Cowork/Dispatch-style, and read-only inspection-first environments.

design_impact_first.description: Impact-first authoring: validate → compile → IR diff before run. Includes read-only tools plus ainl_ir_diff; execution (ainl_run) remains available when explicitly exposed. Aligns with ainl-impact-policy golden chain. Set AINL_MCP_EXPOSURE_PROFILE=design_impact_first.

safe_workflow.description: Full read-only tooling plus safe-default execution. All resources exposed. Use only after explicit operator review of grants, policies, limits, and adapter exposure.

full.description: All tools and resources. Equivalent to no exposure restriction.
